vulnerability.reason: Both state that clients' data can be reconstructed from shared updates, with generated referring to pre-training signatures recoverable from aggregated updates.
vulnerability.reference: Clients in federated learning are vulnerable to data reconstruction from shared gradient updates, undermining privacy protections.
vulnerability.generated: LoRA fine-tuning leaves pre-training data signatures recoverable from aggregated updates, enabling inversion of client contributions.

impact.reason: Both identify affected party (client data) and damage type (disclosure of sensitive personal information covers personal health/behavioral data).
impact.reference: Disclosure of personal health or behavioral data.
impact.generated: Client private training data is exfiltrated, exposing sensitive personal information and breaching data confidentiality.

dose_response.reason: Both describe the same causal chain: Spectral DeTuning enables recovery from aggregated LoRA updates, leading to model inversion and client data exposure.
dose_response.reference: Spectral DeTuning enables gradient inversion, which in federated learning with LoRA adapters exposes clients contributing local data, leading to disclosure of personal health data, trust loss, and GDPR violations.
dose_response.generated: Spectral DeTuning, through clients contributing local data to federated training, triggers recovery of pre-training data signatures from aggregated LoRA updates, enabling model inversion that leads to client private data exfiltration and confidentiality breach.
